description: WooCommerce is a free, open‑source WordPress plugin that turns a website into a fully functional online store, offering product listings, cart, checkout, payment gateways, shipping options, and inventory management.
authentication: OAuth 2.0 (via WooCommerce REST API keys) and Basic Auth (consumer key/secret)
self_serve: Yes – users can install the plugin from the WordPress plugin repository, configure store settings, and manage products directly through the WordPress admin dashboard.
api_surface: REST API v3 (products, orders, customers, coupons, etc.), Webhooks, GraphQL (via WooCommerce GraphQL plugin), XML-RPC
buildability: High – provides REST endpoints, SDKs for PHP, JavaScript, Python, and official client libraries; supports custom extensions via hooks and filters.
main_blocker: Limited to WordPress hosting; requires a WordPress site and server resources; API rate limits and authentication complexity can hinder rapid integration.

description: Xero is a cloud-based accounting software designed for small and medium-sized businesses, offering invoicing, bank reconciliation, inventory management, and financial reporting.
authentication: OAuth 2.0 (client credentials and authorization code flows)
self_serve: Yes – developers can register and obtain API keys via the Xero Developer portal
api_surface: RESTful API with JSON payloads, supporting CRUD operations for contacts, invoices, payments, bank transactions, and more
mcp: Xero App Marketplace (third‑party integrations can be listed and installed by users)
buildability: High – comprehensive SDKs, extensive documentation, sandbox environment, and sample code available
main_blocker: Rate limits (e.g., 100 requests per minute per app) and the requirement to use OAuth 2.0 can add complexity

description: Unknown
authentication: Unknown
self_serve: Unknown
api_surface: Unknown
buildability: Unknown
main_blocker: Unknown

description: Zendesk is a cloud‑based customer service platform that provides ticketing, self‑service portals, knowledge bases, and analytics to help businesses manage customer interactions across multiple channels.
authentication: OAuth 2.0 (client credentials and authorization code flows) and API token authentication are supported for REST API access.
self_serve: Yes – Zendesk offers a self‑service portal and knowledge base that customers can use to find answers and submit tickets without agent intervention.
api_surface: REST API with extensive endpoints for tickets, users, organizations, and more; GraphQL API for flexible queries; Webhooks for event notifications; SDKs for JavaScript, Ruby, Python, and others.
mcp: No – Zendesk does not provide a managed container platform; it is a SaaS application hosted by Zendesk.
buildability: High – developers can build custom integrations using REST/GraphQL APIs, webhooks, and SDKs; Zapier and Integromat connectors are also available.
main_blocker: Limited to the features exposed by the public APIs; certain advanced functionalities (e.g., full ticket history export) may require higher‑tier plans or additional permissions.

description: Zoho Cliq is a real‑time messaging and collaboration platform that offers chat, video calls, file sharing, and integration with other Zoho and third‑party apps.
authentication: OAuth 2.0 (client credentials and authorization code flows)
self_serve: Yes – free tier available with limited features; paid plans unlock additional storage, admin controls, and advanced integrations
api_surface: REST API for creating and managing channels, sending and retrieving messages, managing users, and accessing file attachments. API endpoints are documented under the Zoho Cliq API section.
buildability: Supported – Zoho provides SDKs for Java, Python, Node.js, and .NET, as well as a comprehensive API reference and sample code.
main_blocker: Limited API coverage for certain advanced features (e.g., video call controls, detailed analytics) and rate limits may affect large‑scale integrations.

description: Zoho CRM is a cloud‑based customer relationship management platform that helps businesses manage leads, contacts, deals, and customer interactions.
authentication: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code Grant and Client Credentials Grant)
self_serve: Yes – users can sign up for a free trial or paid plan directly on the Zoho website.
api_surface: RESTful API v2 with endpoints for Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Deals, Campaigns, and more. Supports CRUD operations, search, and batch requests.
buildability: High – well‑documented REST API, SDKs available for multiple languages, and extensive developer resources.
main_blocker: Rate limiting (typically 100 requests per minute per user) and the requirement to complete the OAuth 2.0 flow with redirect URIs can add complexity to integration.
